entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop species being studied for drought adaptation.

entities.name: STAY-GREEN
entities.description: A trait contributing to the adaptation of sorghum to postflowering drought conditions.

entities.name: POSTFLOWERING DROUGHT
entities.description: Environmental stress occurring after the flowering stage.

entities.name: B35
entities.description: The stay-green donor line used for QTL introgression.

entities.name: QTL INTROGRESSION
entities.description: The process of introducing specific quantitative trait loci into different genetic backgrounds.

entities.name: S35
entities.description: A recipient genetic background used for generating introgression lines.

entities.name: R16
entities.description: A recipient genetic background used for generating introgression lines.

entities.name: POST-RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The specific growing season during which the evaluation took place.

entities.name: WELL-WATERED
entities.description: A growing condition with sufficient water availability.

entities.name: WATER-STRESSED
entities.description: A growing condition characterized by limited water availability.

entities.name: GREEN LEAF AREA RETENTION AT MATURITY
entities.description: A measure of the stay-green trait, also referred to as GLAM.

entities.name: STG3B
entities.description: A key stay-green quantitative trait locus associated with improved yield and GLAM.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area.

entities.name: TOTAL DRY MATTER
entities.description: The total biomass produced by the plant.

entities.name: SOIL MOISTURE
entities.description: The amount of water held in the soil, affecting the expression of QTLs.
